title: bursera graveolens subsp. graveolens
intro: Bursera graveolens subsp. graveolens, also known as Palo Santo, is a plant species native to South America, characterized by its unique, aromatic wood and resinous sap. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to have therapeutic effects on various health conditions. The health benefits of Palo Santo include reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory issues, and acting as a natural insect repellent, making it a popular choice in aromatherapy and herbal medicine. The plant is often prepared into herbal preparations such as essential oils, incense sticks, and tea, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system.
